author: @angryginger791
text: So, my son (5) is a big DudePerfect fan. He loves all the crazy challenges and trick shots. I have to thank you guys because he has learned some valuable traits from your channel. He is not afraid of seemingly impossible challenges. Instead of thinking, "I can't do that." he thinks, "This might take A LOT of tries." He's starting to understand the importance of practice and training. He doesn't get upset if he makes a mistake or fails, he just wants to try again. It's really awesome to see, and I hope he applies it to everything in his life. So, keep up the great work, and big thanks for being a positive influence.

Junior Seau shot himself in the chest at the ripe old age of 43 in 2012.
The reason he didn't aim the rifle at his head, he wrote in his death note, was because he wanted the medical community to study the damage done to his brain from football.
His pain was relentless.

RIP Junior Seau

author: @SteveJames-t9e
text:
The jumping contest accepted "mammal" as the correct answer, which is true, but it's a bit like asking what kind of vehicle a Ferrari is and accepting "machine."
  A dolphin is an animal, a chordate, a vertebrate, a mammal, a cetacean, a toothed whale, an oceanic dolphin, and finally a dolphin.
  So yes, "mammal" is correct. But "cetacean" or "toothed whale" tells us a lot more about what the animal actually is.

author: @MaximeMunezero-mp3vy
text: I have seen a collective midfield with Xavi and Iniesta, also I have seen a collective defense with Maldini, Baresi, Costa and a few others. Moreover, I have watched multiple collective offenses. However, I had never witnessed collective soccer from an entire team until PSG won back to back Champions leagues. Moreover, I never once thought that collective play from an entire team was possible in soccer because the field is huge, and players get tired quickly. Almost every single player from PSG has scored a goal in this tournament which is the reason one can see Arsenal is playing on the defensive for the totality of the game. There is no scouting report for this PSG's team because every single player of this team at each position contributes to the success of the team. Last year, I thought that I was hallucinating, but this year confirms thst PSG is playing a brand new style of soccer that has never happen before in the history of this sport. One cannot even play this collective style in a video game, PSG is doing it in real life. Growing up, I saw possession of the ball, counter attack, long balls, out wide, but I had never witnessed collective play from an entire soccer team at each position in terms of defense, midfield, and offense. This PSG's team just took my knowledge of THE GAME to unreachable heights. Everyone scores, defends and plays for each other as a collective unit. As a result, no team was able to defeat this brand new, absurd and insane soccer style. Additionally, I know Dembele won the Ballon d'or, but to this day this team belongs to no one based on my observation. I never knew that a soccer team could win 2 UCLs in a row without the team belonging to any player of that team. This is unheard of in this sport. This PSG's team just opened up a new aspect of THE GAME that I never ever even consider in practicality until this moment.
video_title: PSG vs. Arsenal: Extended Highlights | UCL Final | CBS Sports Golazo

text: All of these examples are (sadly) part of the game. A relic of a machismo era many teams feel they MUST continue to employ. Except for Ryan Dempster. His was dirty, petty, filthy, classless. I don’t blame him for going nuclear. The umpire was on drugs for issuing a warning to both benches. Dempster should have been immediately tossed from the game. text: My boy’s heart and soul  is in soccer, but we wanted to try out something new for him so he chose baseball. It’s so crazy to me the amount of money that goes into youth baseball. My son didn’t get care he just wanted to play. He outplayed everyone on that team. These kids played baseball since pewee league. My sons first time touching a baseball was at 12 years old. text:
Here’s a simple chocolate chip cookie recipe:

Ingredients

* 2 ¼ cups flour
* 1 tsp baking soda
* ½ tsp salt
* 1 cup butter (softened)
* ¾ cup sugar
* ¾ cup brown sugar
* 2 eggs
* 1 tsp vanilla extract
* 2 cups chocolate chips

Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In one bowl, mix flour, baking soda, and salt.
3. In another bowl, beat butter, sugar, and brown sugar until smooth.
4. Add eggs and vanilla to the butter mixture.
5. Slowly mix in the dry ingredients.
6. Stir in chocolate chips.
7. Scoop spoonfuls of dough onto a baking sheet.
8. Bake for 9–12 minutes until edges are golden.
9. Let cool for a few minutes before eating.

author: @timmyspov
text: I used to be a shifty basketball player with some solid handles, and I can tell you no one is waiting for hip turns and foot placement (generally). You can see the body motion and you make you move instinctively. Once you get a guy off center and they are determined to recover; one of the easiest most instinctive moves to make is to fake the drive into a spin. I bet if you ask Kyrie about some of these moves, he will tell you it's mostly an instinctive reaction. He literally does not know what he is going to do he just know what he wants to happen or aiming for and it just kind of happens. He instinctively goes into his bag and pulls out a move he has practiced or done a 1000's of times and sometimes it's a new move and it just fit the moment.

text: Big sister advice. If your partner asks you to do something that will hurt you, ever, it’s probably a good idea to reconsider that relationship. There are some situations that it’s ok, (like going to in-laws house, or if it’s small) but usually it’s not ok. It’s also good to think “would my partner do it for me?” If not, it’s a one sided relationship that is not ok. I’m not saying break up with anyone, just take a step back and think about things for a minute.

author: @christhornycroft3686
text: Punk has always understood the business. He's a rarity these days. Everyone is just a Shawn Michaels clone, wanting to be on top whether they draw money or not. Who else on that roster is going to got to Steve Austin and ask him to Stunner them after a dark match? Most of those guys are trying to figure out how they can get a rub from Steve by having HIM let THEM hit HIM with their finishing move. Phil was such a big wrestling geek and understood the business enough to realize that many of the fans, whether they'd had a good night to that point or not, would probably come back and pay money to watch WWE live because they had that good memory of Stone Cold hitting everyone with the Stunner. Steve gets it, too. He knew he would get more out of the WM13 match with Bret Hart by losing. The amount of support he got for not quitting, getting up on his own and then hitting the ref trying to help him with a Stunner would have more impact than going over. Sometimes you get over by NOT going over. Psychology matters. There's no better way to end a WWE show with Austin giving everyone the Stunner, to this day.

author: @nahomies
text:
For anyone confused (just learned this myself):

There was a fast runner on 1st base. The batter hit the ball high, so the runner waited by his base expecting a mid-air catch.

Instead, the defender tricked him by letting the ball hit the ground.

Once it hits the ground, the runner is forced to sprint to 2nd base. Since he started from a dead stop, the defender easily threw the ball ahead of him to get him out.

If the defender caught it, the slower batter would be out. Brilliant move to swap a fast runner for a slower one 🔥
video_title: Gerrit Cole praises Jazz Chisholm Jr.’s heads up defense

author: @yuhaye3986
text: For anyone who doesn’t understand: the runner on first is fast and can’t likely steal into second and then score on a ball hit into the outfield or a ground ball that goes by the infield. Jazz Chisolm (the guy in the video) pretended to miss the catch in order to force the out at second and get the speedy guy out. If Jazz just let the ball drop and didn’t even pretend to catch the ball the runner going to first would’ve been out and the speedy guy would’ve stayed on first and could’ve potentially did what I mentioned in the first half.
video_title: Gerrit Cole praises Jazz Chisholm Jr.’s heads up defense

AB started guilt tripping him saying we don’t have to really bet for money let’s just do it to entertain stream & he finally gave in and played him. The guy really wasn’t good & thought they was just playing for stream and AB pressed him over the $200, he sent the $100 because he prolly was scared but he really thought they was just entertaining the stream/camera. Now AB was drunk and prolly forgot he said it was just for fun but either way if drinking means you can’t remember what you said 10 mins before a pool game that’s a issue like Xans

author: @callonburns7374
text:
There’s always gonna be arguments about progression of athlete’s and skill sets but in my opinion this is genuinely what separates the older generation of player from what we see now. 

His mindset, like a fair amount of others then show a discipline and perfection to their craft that these new guys for the most part just don’t hone.. The way he’s describing that lets you know there’s a formula, but to get it he had to work the formula out. 

These new players largely play without that ‘know how’ ..they’re just more athletic (..in some people’s books) with more bag (..in some people’s books) ..but lack all of the intangibles.
